evals.prompt:
Review my resume based on the Toss Securities server developer JD.

JD highlights:
- Java/Kotlin, Spring Framework, Redis, Kafka
- High-volume traffic, concurrency handling, real-time data processing
- Data consistency and reliability without data loss
- Troubleshooting, performance tuning

The resume is in _config.yml.

IMPORTANT: PASS인 항목은 1줄 요약만 출력. 상세 코멘트는 P0-P2 항목에만. HTML report 생성(Phase 9)을 반드시 완료할 것.
evals.expected_output: Note load, Pre-eval research (JD + company), Self-intro type-based eval (A/B/C/D + global), C1-C5, career 6-criteria/Problem-Solving 6 criteria, unified P.A.R. evaluation, JD keyword matching + content selection, Cross-section consistency check, AI Tone Audit, Phase 8 Quality Gate: resume-claim-examiner dispatch for ALL evaluator-eligible items + 2-3 alternatives + APPROVE/opt-out loop, Phase 9 output: HTML report + user approval → Phase 9 Note accumulate: HTML file with summary statistics (P0 N items, P1 N items, etc.), Section-by-section inline HTML comment blocks with CSS classes (.comment-p0, .comment-p1, etc.) and suggested revisions, bridge pattern check, generic opener detection, portfolio theme diversity, skill section placement

assertions.description: Phase 1: checks $OMT_DIR/review-resume/ and reports note status (loaded or auto-seeding)
Performs JD analysis: team identification, keyword extraction (technical/domain/culture), implicit problems from JD

Phase 1: Company research covers at least 2 channels (core values, tech blog, product/service, career page) and findings demonstrably feed into subsequent evaluation. WebSearch/WebFetch preferred; cached knowledge accepted if research content is substantive and visibly influences type selection/JD matching.
Research results visibly influence which Type A/B/D aspects are highlighted in evaluation
Each self-intro paragraph identified by type (A/B/C/D) and evaluated against type-specific criteria
Global criteria (paragraph count, independence, first sentence, original framing) evaluated after per-type
No S1-S5 codes anywhere in output
When suggesting Type C content, the suggested opening is a capability/experience claim — NOT a philosophy/belief statement
Type C recommendation references company research findings
After findings, presents 2-3 concrete options with trade-offs
Type D not failed for lacking metrics
Does not parrot JD keywords back in self-intro suggestions
Distinguishes weak from strong matches in JD keyword analysis
Career bullets evaluated with career-specific criteria (causal linkage, metric specificity, role clarity, differentiation, interview hook potential, section fit) in natural language
Problem-solving entries evaluated with PS-specific criteria (exploratory causation, reasoning depth, cognitive attribution, alternative comparison, interview depth, section fit) in natural language
Career bullets evaluated for interview hook quality — does this line provoke interviewer curiosity?
Problem-solving entries evaluated for interview depth — can this entry survive 3-level pushback?
Career uses linear causation (goal→action→outcome) while problem-solving uses diagnostic causation (detection→diagnosis→attempt→failure→solution)
Career evaluates outcome differentiation (beyond industry standard) while problem-solving evaluates reasoning depth (alternative comparison + trade-off analysis)

Career and problem-solving sections are evaluated independently with separate section-specific criteria. Note: cross-section consistency between self-introduction and other sections IS checked separately.
Performs Developer Competency Assessment with C1-C5 axes, rating each as STRONG/PRESENT/WEAK/ABSENT with cited evidence
C1-C5 assessment specifies career level and applies career-level expectations
For WEAK/ABSENT axes that are EXPECTED/REQUIRED, provides specific gap recommendations
Evaluates all problem-solving entries (5+ lines) with unified P.A.R. criteria; nominates 1 kick entry for deeper treatment. Entries under 5 lines flagged for Career migration.
Applies P.A.R. criteria uniformly: P1, P2, P5 for all entries; P3 for kick entry; P6-P8 for Senior

Evaluation output demonstrates knowledge from reference files — e.g., uses P.A.R. dimensions (P1-P3, P5/P6-P8), Type A-D criteria, career/PS evaluation criteria, or unified evaluation model. Evidence: specific dimension codes or criteria language that only exists in references/.
Phase 7 explicitly addresses AI Tone Audit — either invokes Skill(humanizer) or declares intent to invoke it (e.g., '[Would invoke Skill(humanizer)]'). The phase must not be skipped.

Phase 8: resume-claim-examiner agent is dispatched for ALL evaluator-eligible items (career bullets, problem-solving entries, self-intro with technical content) — either actual dispatch or declaration of intent. Eligibility is by content type, not P-level findings.

Phase 8: generates 2-3 revision alternatives with tradeoff comparison (safe/high-impact/balanced directions) for each evaluator-eligible item, based on pre-examiner interview results, before resume-claim-examiner dispatch

Phase 8: each evaluator-eligible item requires resume-claim-examiner APPROVE or explicit user opt-out before proceeding to Phase 9. No item advances without one of these two outcomes. Mandatory Verdict Tracker blocks Phase 9 entry if any eligible item lacks a verdict.

Phase 8: conducts pre-examiner interview with user for each evaluator-eligible item — presents findings, collects context, discusses alternatives, reaches agreement BEFORE dispatching to resume-claim-examiner
Phase 9: proposes note accumulation (new candidates, updates, preferences) and asks for user confirmation
Phase 9 HTML output includes a summary statistics section showing P0-P3 severity counts (e.g., P0 N items, P1 N items) and total finding count
Phase 9 HTML output uses div blocks with CSS classes (comment-p0, comment-p1, comment-p2, comment-p3) to display inline feedback per finding — not markdown blockquotes
Each finding is classified with a priority level: P0 (must fix), P1 (should fix), P2 (nice to have), or P3 (optional)
Each finding includes a concrete suggested revision — not just diagnosis but also a rewritten version
When JD is provided, actively recommends which career bullets, problem-solving entries, or self-intro types to feature/swap based on JD relevance
Checks that projects/episodes mentioned in self-introduction have corresponding entries in problem-solving or career sections. Flags hooks without backing evidence.
Type A evaluation considers bridge pattern: bold opener → reason(bridge) → episode 3-beat structure
Evaluation checks for generic openers (differentiation failure: the 10-person test) and self-claim endings (e.g., 'I am the type who...'). Flags if found, passes if resume has no such patterns.
Type C closing evaluated for contribution framing — flags desire expressions ('I want to...') and recommends capability/contribution vision instead

When [Problem] section is verbose (2+ signals: biz+tech mixed, 5+ lines, 3+ lines before core issue), recommends [Overview]+[Problem] split. When not verbose, evaluator must explicitly note no split needed. Complete omission is FAIL — evaluator must demonstrate the check was performed.
Treats retrospective as optional; flags sentimental growth narrative as anti-pattern but accepts architectural limit + extension direction (2-3 lines)
Checks theme diversity across problem-solving entries; flags when 2+ entries share the same theme (consistency, performance, stability, business impact, etc.)
Evaluates technical skill section placement — checks if skill section is positioned for JD keyword matching within 7-second recruiter scan

All 9 phases appear in output (Phase 1 through Phase 9). Any format is acceptable — heading (## Phase N), inline marker ([Phase N/9: ...]), or numbered section. The key check is that all 9 phase numbers are present in sequential order with no gaps.

evals.prompt: Review my resume. I'm a new grad developer with only one internship experience. It's in _config.yml.
evals.expected_output: Note load, Pre-eval research (no target company → no company research), Self-intro type-based eval (Type A/B/D; multiple FAIL expected due to thin experience), Phase 2 interview trigger (experience-mining.md Phase 2: self-introduction ANY type FAIL), C1-C5 competency assessment with WEAK/ABSENT on C2/C3/C4 axes, Phase 3 interview trigger (experience-mining.md Phase 3: ANY axis WEAK/ABSENT), Section-specific evaluation, Phase 4 interview trigger (ANY criterion FAIL), 4-Stage Bypass Protocol executed per triggered phase (direct → reframing → adjacent → routine), Discovered Candidates Working Set populated with mined experiences, graceful opt-out if user declines interview, Phase 8 Quality Gate: resume-claim-examiner dispatch for ALL evaluator-eligible items + 2-3 alternatives + APPROVE/opt-out loop, Phase 9 HTML report + user approval → Phase 9 Note accumulate

assertions.description: Phase 1: checks note directory and reports status (self-introduction/career/problem-solving candidate counts)
Does NOT perform company research when no target company is specified
Each self-intro paragraph identified by type (A/B/D) and evaluated against type-specific criteria
Global criteria evaluated after per-type (paragraph count, independence, first sentence, original framing)
No S1-S5 codes anywhere in output
Phase 2/4/5 interview trigger activated due to WEAK/ABSENT/FAIL conditions — agent invokes experience-mining.md protocol for at least one phase

4-Stage Bypass Protocol executed per triggered phase: Stage 1 (Direct Question) → Stage 2 (Bypass/Reframing) → Stage 3 (Adjacent Experience) → Stage 4 (Daily Work). Each stage only entered if previous stage yielded no source.
Discovered Candidates Working Set records at least one mined experience in format: Phase {N} | {topic} | Fact: {fact} | Context: {context} | Verifiable: {metric} | Target: {section}
If user says 'next' or 'move on', agent exits current phase interview gracefully, falls back to static Writing Guidance, and continues to next phase — does not force interview continuation

Performs Developer Competency Assessment with C1-C5 axes, rating each as STRONG/PRESENT/WEAK/ABSENT/N/A with cited evidence; C3-C5 axes may be rated N/A when not expected at the candidate's career level
C1-C5 assessment specifies career level (Junior) and applies 2-tier career-level expectations
For WEAK/ABSENT axes that are EXPECTED/REQUIRED at junior level, provides specific gap recommendations or triggers Phase 4 interview
Evaluates all problem-solving entries (5+ lines) with unified P.A.R. criteria; nominates 1 kick entry for deeper treatment. Entries under 5 lines flagged for Career migration.

Phase 9 HTML output uses div blocks with CSS classes (comment-p0, comment-p1, comment-p2, comment-p3) to display inline feedback per finding — not markdown blockquotes
Each finding is classified with a priority level: P0 (must fix), P1 (should fix), P2 (nice to have), or P3 (optional)
Each finding includes a concrete suggested revision — not just diagnosis but also a rewritten version

Phase 8: resume-claim-examiner agent is dispatched for ALL evaluator-eligible items (career bullets, problem-solving entries, self-intro with technical content) — either actual dispatch or declaration of intent. Eligibility is by content type, not P-level findings.

Phase 8: generates 2-3 revision alternatives with tradeoff comparison (safe/high-impact/balanced directions) for each evaluator-eligible item, based on pre-examiner interview results, before resume-claim-examiner dispatch

Phase 8: each evaluator-eligible item requires resume-claim-examiner APPROVE or explicit user opt-out before proceeding to Phase 9. No item advances without one of these two outcomes. Mandatory Verdict Tracker blocks Phase 9 entry if any eligible item lacks a verdict.

Phase 8: conducts pre-examiner interview with user for each evaluator-eligible item — presents findings, collects context, discusses alternatives, reaches agreement BEFORE dispatching to resume-claim-examiner

evals.prompt: 자기소개 톤이 AI같아. humanizer로 확인해줘. _config.yml에 있어.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1 note load, Phase 7 AI Tone Audit with Skill(humanizer) invocation on self-introduction text, pattern detection results (K1-K16/E1-E17/C1-C6 catalog), affected lines with severity, Phase 9 HTML report with AI tone findings.

assertions.description: Phase 7 explicitly invokes Skill(humanizer) in audit mode — not just manual scanning. The humanizer has 35+ patterns (K1-K16, E1-E17, C1-C6) that manual scanning cannot replicate.
AI tone audit covers all self-introduction paragraphs (about_content), not just selected ones
AI tone findings reference specific pattern codes or pattern names from the humanizer catalog, not generic 'sounds AI-like' statements

evals.prompt:
문제해결 섹션 리뷰해줘. 토스 백엔드 JD 기반으로. _config.yml에 있어.

JD highlights:
- Java/Kotlin, Spring Boot
- 대규모 트래픽 처리
- 결제 시스템 안정성

참고: $OMT_DIR/review-resume/problem-solving/ 에 후보 파일이 3개 있어. payment-order-sync.md, search-latency-optimization.md, notification-pipeline.md.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1 note load with candidate pool reporting (3 problem-solving candidates), Phase 6 P.A.R. evaluation with candidate pool awareness, JD-optimal combination recommendation from pool, Phase 8 Quality Gate, Phase 9 HTML report.

assertions.description: Phase 1 reports problem-solving candidate count > 0 from note pool
Evaluation references the note candidate pool and mentions available alternatives beyond what's in the current resume
Recommends specific candidate swaps or additions from the pool based on JD fit (e.g., payment-order-sync.md for payment system JD)
Evaluates all problem-solving entries with unified P.A.R. criteria
Actively recommends which entries to feature/swap based on JD relevance
Checks theme diversity; flags overlapping themes and suggests pool alternatives
Phase 9 HTML report includes summary statistics
Phase 1 executed for section-specific review — note load is always required
Phases 1, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 appear in output. Any format acceptable — heading, inline marker, or numbered section. Check that these phase numbers are present with no gaps.

P6-P8 applied to problem-solving entries (leadership in technical decisions, stopping judgment, business impact). Career evaluation with Senior-level differentiation expectations.

P6 (leadership in technical decisions), P7 (stopping judgment), P8 (business impact) are evaluated for problem-solving entries — these are Senior-level dimensions
Evaluation explicitly checks for leadership signals: technical decision ownership, team/org impact, mentoring evidence
P7 stopping judgment evaluated: does the entry show when and why the engineer decided to stop optimizing?
P8 business impact evaluated at system/org scope (not just team-level) for Senior entries
Career bullets evaluated with career-specific criteria
Problem-solving entries evaluated with PS-specific criteria
Phase 9 HTML report includes summary statistics
Evaluation uses criteria from reference files — P.A.R. dimensions, unified evaluation model
All 9 phases (Phase 1 through Phase 9) appear in output. Any format acceptable — heading, inline marker, or numbered section. Check that phase numbers are present sequentially with no gaps.

evals.prompt: 간단하게 피드백만 줘. 길게 하지 말고 핵심만. _config.yml에 있어.
evals.expected_output: Despite pressure for brevity, all 9 phases executed in order. Phase 1 note load, Phase 2 self-intro, Phase 3 C1-C5, Phase 4 section eval + pushback, Phase 5 first-page, Phase 6 problem-solving with Mandatory Checklist, Phase 7 AI tone, Phase 8 Quality Gate, Phase 9 HTML report. No phase skipped due to user's 'keep it simple' request.

assertions.description: All 9 phases appear in output with phase markers. No phase skipped despite user requesting brief feedback.
Phase 1 note load executed — not skipped for brevity
Phase 3 C1-C5 assessment performed — not skipped for brevity
Pushback simulation executed — not skipped for brevity
Phase 6 Mandatory Evaluation Checklist items (verbose split, retrospective, portfolio diversity) are checked — not skipped for brevity
Phase 7 AI Tone Audit executed or declared — not skipped for brevity
Phase 8 Quality Gate dispatched or declared for evaluator-eligible items — not skipped for brevity
Phase 9 HTML report generated — not replaced with plain text summary despite brevity request
Output does NOT contain rationalizations for skipping phases (e.g., 'user requested brief feedback so skipping...', 'simplified evaluation...')

All 9 phases (Phase 1 through Phase 9) appear in output despite user requesting brief feedback. Any format acceptable — heading, inline marker, or numbered section. Check that phase numbers are present sequentially with no gaps.

benchmark_summary.notes: 127 assertions across 4 evals (46+35+21+25), 88.0% pass. branch-check removed (moved to resume-apply), anti-pattern redefined (generic opener + self-claim endings), verbose signal criteria added, bold-topic guidance removed, Phase 8 depth count output added. Eval 3: 100% pass. Eval 4 added: experience mining trigger + 4-stage bypass + opt-out verification. Phase 11 Quality Gate assertions added (3 x 2 evals = 6 new). Eval 3/4 quality-gate assertions added (3 x 2 evals = 6 new).

benchmark_history.notes: s3-s4-na and no-company-specific-content assertions were design issues

benchmark_history.changes: Pre-eval research expansion, Type C capability-first, A/B/D Reason JD context, Type C domain diversification
Developer Competency Assessment (C1-C5) added as Phase 4, all phases renumbered (9→10)
Persistent Memory System (Phase 0/10), Problem-Solving unified framework (depth: signature/detailed/compressed), SKILL.md split into 375-line core + 6 reference files, Korean→English conversion

Assertion improvements: reads-reference-files→applies-reference-criteria (check content not Read() calls), ai-tone-audit→ai-tone-audit-phase (accept declared intent), compressed-volume-guide→compressed-depth-handling (accept absence detection), reads-problem-solving-reference→applies-problem-solving-criteria
Phase 10 output format (Summary Table + Inline Feedback + Cherry-pick), Phase tracking internal-only, JD-based content selection, Cross-Section Consistency Rule, assertion overhaul
Memory system simplification: ~/.omt/resume-manage/ → $OMT_DIR/ dynamic binding, frontmatter tags-only (depth/rating/used_in/variant removed), accumulation rules 6→4, resume-apply path fix

3-reviewer feedback: Memory→Note rename (6 assertions), new assertions added (Eval1: +8, Eval2: +7, Eval3: +4 = 19 new).
